alerts.title: Iran suspends US nuclear talks after Israeli strikes on Lebanon escalate
alerts.summary: Iranian officials announced suspension of nuclear negotiations with the US following Israeli military strikes on southern Lebanon and Beirut suburbs (lat 34.08, lon 35.68). NBC News reports Tehran cited Israeli attacks as reason for halting dialogue. Development occurs amid active Persian Gulf hostilities and 60% uranium enrichment status, effectively removing diplomatic off-ramp during peak military tension.

alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment at 60% U-235; 1-2 week breakout window active
alerts.summary: Global metrics confirm Iranian enrichment sustained at 60% U-235 with estimated 1-2 week nuclear breakout timeline. Kazakhstan continues offering to host Iranian uranium stockpile as diplomatic pressure point (Reuters, Euronews, Financial Times). Breakout risk critical following ceasefire collapse and suspension of US-Iran talks as of June 3 UTC.

alerts.title: US strikes Qeshm Island radar sites; Iran targets Fifth Fleet HQ in Kuwait
alerts.summary: CENTCOM confirmed US self-defense strikes on Iranian radar sites and communications infrastructure on Qeshm Island (lat 32.3, lon 52.95) in response to Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks. Iran's IRGC claimed strikes on US Fifth Fleet headquarters and airbase near Kuwait (lat 31.73, lon 52.87). Multiple incidents geo-clustered in Persian Gulf confirm sustained military exchange; ceasefire collapsed.
alerts.region: Persian Gulf / Strait of Hormuz / Kuwait

alerts.title: Two US carrier strike groups positioned in Arabian Sea and Red Sea theater
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Abraham Lincoln (Carrier) deployed in Arabian Sea (lat 20.65, lon 59.65) and USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Carrier) in Red Sea (lat 15.61, lon 42.42), supported by destroyers USS Carney, USS Mason, USS Laboon, and amphibious assault ship USS Bataan. Dual-carrier deployment maintains forward presence amid active hostilities and nuclear escalation risks.
alerts.region: Arabian Sea / Red Sea / Persian Gulf

alerts.title: US negative sentiment spike on Iran conflict amid domestic political noise
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows US negative sentiment at 49% (vs. 42% positive) with 'iran' and 'strikes' keywords surging in prominence. Word cloud dominated by 'trump' (59 mentions), 'iran' (23), and primary election terms, indicating conflict coverage competing with domestic political coverage. Spike in military-related keywords ('strikes,' 'airstrike') reflects escalation narrative penetration in American discourse.

analysis_note: The US-Iran conflict has entered a critical escalation phase with active kinetic operations in the Persian Gulf, suspension of nuclear talks, and Iranian enrichment at 60% U-235 creating a 1-2 week breakout window—while dual US carrier strike groups remain forward-deployed and threat level remains at 9/10 CRITICAL.
